children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Beginning , the Secretary may award Community College and Career Training Grants to eligible institutions for the purpose of developing, offering, or improving educational or career training programs for workers eligible for training under .August 1, 2009section 2296 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: more than one grant under this section; or

children.label: B
children.text: a grant under this section in excess of $1,000,000.

children.heading: Eligible institution
children.label: 1 Eligible institution
children.text: The term “eligible institution” means an institution of higher education (as defined in ), but only with respect to a program offered by the institution that can be completed in not more than 2 years.section 1002 of title 20

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 2 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Labor.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An eligible institution seeking to receive a grant under this section shall submit a grant proposal to the Secretary at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary may require.

children.label: A
children.text: promulgate guidelines for the submission of grant proposals under this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: publish and maintain such guidelines on the website of the Department of Labor.

children.heading: Assistance
children.label: 3 Assistance
children.text: The Secretary shall offer assistance in preparing a grant proposal to any eligible institution that requests such assistance.

children.label: i
children.text: the specific project for which the grant proposal is submitted, including the manner in which the grant will be used to develop, offer, or improve an educational or career training program that is suited to workers eligible for training under ;section 2296 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the extent to which the project for which the grant proposal is submitted will meet the educational or career training needs of workers in the community served by the eligible institution who are eligible for training under ; andsection 2296 of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: any previous experience of the eligible institution in providing educational or career training programs to workers eligible for training under .section 2296 of this title

children.heading: Absence of experience
children.label: B Absence of experience
children.text: The absence of any previous experience in providing educational or career training programs described in subparagraph (A)(iii) shall not automatically disqualify an eligible institution from receiving a grant under this section.

children.label: I
children.text: any shortcomings in existing educational and career training opportunities available to workers in the community; and

children.label: II
children.text: any future employment opportunities within the community and the educational and career training skills required for workers to meet the future employment demand; and

children.label: ii
children.text: reached out to other similarly situated institutions in an effort to benefit from any best practices that may be shared with respect to providing educational or career training programs to workers eligible for training under ; andsection 2296 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the extent and outcome of the outreach conducted under subparagraph (A);

children.label: ii
children.text: the extent to which the project for which the grant proposal is submitted will contribute to meeting any shortcomings identified under subparagraph (A)(i)(I) or any educational or career training needs identified under subparagraph (A)(i)(II); and

children.label: iii
children.text: the extent to which employers, including small- and medium-sized firms within the community, have demonstrated a commitment to employing workers who would benefit from the project for which the grant proposal is submitted.

children.heading: Criteria for award of grants
children.label: d Criteria for award of grants
A
children.text: a determination of the merits of the grant proposal submitted by the eligible institution to develop, offer, or improve educational or career training programs to be made available to workers eligible for training under ;section 2296 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: an evaluation of the likely employment opportunities available to workers who complete an educational or career training program that the eligible institution proposes to develop, offer, or improve; and

children.label: C
children.text: an evaluation of prior demand for training programs by workers eligible for training under in the community served by the eligible institution, as well as the availability and capacity of existing training programs to meet future demand for training programs.section 2296 of this title

children.heading: Matching requirements
children.label: 2 Matching requirements
children.text: A grant awarded under this section may not be used to satisfy any private matching requirement under any other provision of law.
describing each grant awarded under this section during the preceding fiscal year;

assessing the impact of each award of a grant under this section in a fiscal year preceding the fiscal year referred to in paragraph (1) on workers receiving training under ; andsection 2296 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: Of the grants awarded under this section, the amount of funds spent by grantees.

children.label: B
children.text: The average dollar amount of grants awarded under this section.

children.label: C
children.text: The average duration of grants awarded under this section.

children.label: D
children.text: The percentage of workers receiving benefits under part 2 that are served by programs developed, offered, or improved using grants awarded under this section.

children.label: E
children.text: The percentage and number of workers receiving benefits under part 2 who obtained a degree through such programs and the average duration of the participation of such workers in training under .section 2296 of this title

children.label: F
children.text: The number of workers receiving benefits under part 2 served by such programs who did not complete a degree and the average duration of the participation of such workers in training under .section 2296 of this title
